summary: Initial repository setup for building a full-assembly Melee DOL from a generated disassembly. The PR added a very large asm/disasm.s, a root Makefile using devkitPPC assembler and Metrowerks linker/compiler under Wine, an ldscript.lcf fixing section virtual addresses and SDA bases, an elf2dol conversion tool, SHA1 verification for main.dol, and ignore rules for toolchain/build artifacts.
what_changed: Added asm/disasm.s containing the initial full-game PowerPC assembly dump, with section metadata, register constants, SDA base constants, global function labels such as func_80003100, and address-style local labels such as lbl_800031D8.

Added a root Makefile that builds asm/*.s and src/*.c into objects, links with Metrowerks mwldeppc.exe using ldscript.lcf, converts the resulting ELF to main.dol with tools/elf2dol, and checks the output against ssbm.sha1.
Configured toolchain variables for Wine, devkitPPC powerpc-eabi-as, powerpc-eabi-objcopy, Metrowerks mwcceppc.exe/mwldeppc.exe, and sha1sum.
Added ldscript.lcf binding .text0, .text1, .data0 through .data7, and .bss to explicit runtime addresses, and defining _SDA_BASE_ = 0x804DB6A0 and _SDA2_BASE_ = 0x804DF9E0.

Added tools/elf2dol.c, a C utility that validates a 32-bit big-endian PowerPC executable ELF, reads PT_LOAD program headers, maps executable segments to DOL text slots, non-executable file-backed segments to DOL data slots, folds zero-fill into BSS, aligns DOL payloads to 32 bytes, and writes a DOL header plus segment contents.
Added tools/Makefile to build elf2dol with gcc -O3 -Wall -s.
Added ssbm.sha1 containing expected hash 08e0bf20134dfcb260699671004527b2d6bb1a45 for main.dol.
Added ignore rules for mwcc_compiler/, object/ELF/map/DOL outputs, editor swap files, tool executable outputs, and *.exe.
smart_moves: Made the very first bootstrap build verifiable by running sha1sum -c ssbm.sha1 after producing main.dol, establishing byte-for-byte rebuild checking from the start.
Used a linker script with explicit BIND addresses for each discovered section, preserving the original memory layout while the project was still all assembly.
Included _SDA_BASE_ and _SDA2_BASE_ in both the assembly and linker script context, which is essential for matching PowerPC small-data references.

Ran powerpc-eabi-objcopy on the linked ELF because the Makefile notes that the Metrowerks linker did not generate physical addresses in ELF program headers; this prepared the ELF for elf2dol segment extraction.
Kept the compiler binaries out of git via mwcc_compiler/ in .gitignore while documenting the expected local toolchain path in Makefile variables.
Separated host-side helper tool construction into tools/Makefile so the main build can depend on a tools phony target before DOL conversion.
decomp_lessons: A decomp project can begin with a monolithic generated assembly fallback plus a linker script; C files can then be introduced incrementally while the all-assembly build remains the matching baseline.
For DOL reconstruction, exact section virtual addresses, physical/program-header addresses, entry point, BSS placement, and segment ordering matter as much as instruction text.
Hash verification should be part of the primary build recipe, not an optional manual step, so regressions are caught immediately.
When using Metrowerks tools through Wine with devkitPPC binutils, document every tool variable and flag in the Makefile; future contributors need reproducible assumptions.
The DOL format has limits on text and data segment counts, reflected in elf2dol by MAX_TEXT_SEGMENTS = 7 and MAX_DATA_SEGMENTS = 11; linker section planning must respect those limits.
Generated labels using address-based names such as func_80003100 and lbl_800031D8 are acceptable bootstrap placeholders before semantic naming is known.
naming_conventions: Unknown global functions in the generated assembly use func_XXXXXXXX names based on runtime address, e.g. func_80003100.
Unknown local/data labels use lbl_XXXXXXXX names based on runtime address, e.g. lbl_800031D8.
Linker/disassembly section names use numbered buckets such as .text0, .text1, .data0 through .data7, and .bss.
Small data symbols use standard PowerPC/GameCube names _SDA_BASE_ and _SDA2_BASE_.
Build variables in the Makefile are uppercase descriptive names such as S_FILES, C_FILES, LDSCRIPT, DOL, ELF, MAP, O_FILES, WINE, AS, CC, LD, ELF2DOL, and SHA1SUM.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved original instruction bytes in comments before each assembly instruction, including runtime address and file offset columns, making generated assembly auditable against the source binary.
Defined PowerPC register aliases with .set r0-r31, f0-f31, and qr0-qr7 at the top of asm/disasm.s for assembler compatibility/readability.
Bound each section in ldscript.lcf to its original runtime address rather than relying on linker placement.
Used devkitPPC powerpc-eabi-as with -mgekko and include path support for assembling GameCube PowerPC code.
Converted ELF to DOL by classifying executable PT_LOAD segments as text and non-executable file-backed PT_LOAD segments as data, while treating zero-size-file or memsz-filesz ranges as BSS.
Aligned DOL segment file offsets to 32 bytes in elf2dol.
The Makefile uses Metrowerks flags including -proc gekko, -fp hard, -O2,p, and -Cpp_exceptions off for future C compilation compatibility.
review_feedback: One collaborator review by RevoSucks approved the PR with an empty body; no line-specific review comments or rationale were present in the provided context.
searchable_terms: physical addresses in ELF program headers
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that split asm/disasm.s into smaller files or replace generated assembly with C.
Find later changes to ldscript.lcf section bindings or SDA base definitions.
Find later changes to tools/elf2dol.c or replacement DOL conversion tooling.
Find PRs that modify the Makefile toolchain flags for mwcceppc.exe, mwldeppc.exe, or powerpc-eabi-as.
Find PRs that update ssbm.sha1 or alter the hash verification workflow.
